Part B.key_evidence: Science and Reference both show a resolved source at the center with nearby companions; the difference image shows a noisy, irregular residual rather than a clean point-source dipole. isdiffpos=f indicates the source is fainter in science than reference. sgscore1=0.23 with distpsnr1=1.65 arcsec and a low classtar (0.43) suggest the host is extended/galaxy-like. ndethist=4 over ncovhist=1598 visits indicates rare activity at this position. chinr=7.4 (high) suggests a poorly-fit / extended reference source.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: The combination of a galaxy-like host (low sgscore1, extended morphology, high chinr), only 4 historical detections out of ~1600 visits, and a fading (negative subtraction) PSF detection at mag ~18.9 is most consistent with AGN variability in a resolved host. Repeated low-level variability over many epochs disfavors a one-off supernova, and the host being extended disfavors a stellar variable.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An alternative is a supernova in the host galaxy; however, SNe brighten rather than fade at first detection in a typical scenario, and the alert is a negative subtraction with prior detections in history, which fits AGN better. A stellar variable is unlikely given the extended host morphology and low sgscore1.
